mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 1.16% remains below the neutral rate estimate of 2.5-3.0%, signaling monetary accommodation. This persistent below-neutral stance compresses long-term discount rates and reduces term premium, causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline over the next 20 trading days as markets price in sustained low rates.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.1% is near the estimated natural rate, signaling tight labor markets. Sustained tight labor conditions trigger wage acceleration expectations, which feed into inflation expectations and push 10-year Treasury yields upward as investors demand higher real yields to compensate for expected inflation erosion.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.3% for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: UNRATE rises above 4.5% within 20 trading days, or 10-year Treasury yields fall despite UNRATE remaining below 4.3%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 492,717 million dollars show robust consumer demand. Strong retail sales growth signals sustained economic expansion and inflation pressures, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as markets reprice growth and inflation expectations upward over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 492717.0 million dollars as of 2017-11-15
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 10 basis points over the 20-day window despite RSAFS growth remaining positive, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 106.40 (index 2017=100) indicates strong supply-side capacity utilization. When industrial production accelerates above trend, it signals reduced slack in production capacity and upward pressure on input costs and inflation, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as inflation expectations increase.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls despite INDPRO growth exceeding 0.5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 12-month rolling window, or INDPRO declines in the following month.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 1,290 thousand units combined with payroll employment at 147,241 thousand create a joint demand signal. When both housing construction and employment growth remain robust, aggregate demand pressures intensify, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as markets price in stronger growth and inflation over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1290.0 and PAYEMS at 147241.0 as of 2017-11-15
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 12 basis points despite both HOUST and PAYEMS meeting trigger conditions, or either series declines sharply within 10 trading days, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL at 247.592) combined with accommodative FEDFUNDS (1.16%) creates a negative real rate environment. When real rates remain deeply negative, bond investors demand higher nominal yields to maintain real return targets, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the real rate gap widens.
candidate_trigger: Real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS - inflation) remains below -1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 247.592 and FEDFUNDS at 1.16% as of 2017-11-15
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls despite real rates remaining below -1.0%, or FEDFUNDS rises above 2.0% within 30 trading days, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 147,241 thousand combined with unemployment at 4.1% signals a tight labor market with limited slack. When employment growth accelerates while unemployment remains near cycle lows, wage pressures intensify, triggering inflation expectations that push 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 147241.0 and UNRATE at 4.1% as of 2017-11-15
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 15 basis points over 60 trading days despite both conditions met, or UNRATE rises above 4.5% within 30 trading days, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1 at 17,169.733 billion chained 2017 dollars) combined with retail sales momentum creates a demand-driven expansion signal. When both real output and consumer spending remain elevated, growth expectations rise and 10-year Treasury yields decline as the market reprices the probability of Fed rate cuts in response to potential future slowdown risks.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.3% MoM AND GDPC1 annualized growth trajectory > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17169.733 and RSAFS at 492717.0 as of 2017-11-15
regime_hint: sustained above-trend growth with policy normalization risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 10 basis points despite trigger conditions met, or GDPC1 growth forecast revisions turn negative, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 12-month rolling window.
